---
title: "Project 3 Final Report - Analysis Work"
author: "Madelynn Schina"
date: "April 2026"
output: pdf_document
---


```{r, include=F} 
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(knitr)
library(here)
library(survival)
library(car)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

```


```{r, echo = F}
# Loading dataset
frmgham_data <- read.csv(here("Project 3/Data", "frmgham_data_cleaned.csv"))

# Separating data into male and female
male_data <- frmgham_data %>%
  filter(SEX == 1) %>%
  select(-SEX)

female_data <- frmgham_data %>%
  filter(SEX == 2) %>%
  select(-SEX)

```


```{r, echo = F}
# Checking collinearity of predictors before performing variable selection
cor_test_f <- cor(female_data$SYSBP, as.numeric(female_data$BPMEDS), use = "complete.obs")
cor_test_m <- cor(male_data$SYSBP, as.numeric(male_data$BPMEDS), use = "complete.obs")

vif_model_f <- lm(stroke_10yr ~ SYSBP + BPMEDS + DIABETES + AGE + PREVCHD + CURSMOKE + TOTCHOL + BMI, 
                  data = female_data)
vif_model_m <- lm(stroke_10yr ~ SYSBP + BPMEDS + DIABETES + AGE + PREVCHD + CURSMOKE + TOTCHOL + BMI, 
                  data = male_data)

# Everything looks good, no concerns

```



```{r, echo = F}
# Variable Selection of additional covariates
# To Be Considered: coronary heart disease, blood pressure medicine, smoking status, total cholesterol, bmi

run_variable_selection <- function(data, sex_label) {
  
  candidates <- c("PREVCHD", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE", "TOTCHOL", "BMI")
  forced     <- c("AGE", "SYSBP", "DIABETES")
  all_vars   <- c("stroke_time", "stroke_10yr", forced, candidates)
  
  # Identify complete cases across all variables for consistent sample size
  data_complete <- data[complete.cases(data[, all_vars]), ]
  cat("\n===", sex_label, "- N used for selection:", nrow(data_complete), "===\n")
  
  # Generate all 32 subsets of candidates
  candidate_subsets <- unlist(
    lapply(0:length(candidates), function(k) combn(candidates, k, simplify = FALSE)),
    recursive = FALSE
  )
  
  # Fit all models on the same complete dataset
  fit_model <- function(subset) {
    vars    <- c(forced, subset)
    formula <- as.formula(paste("Surv(stroke_time, stroke_10yr) ~", 
                                paste(vars, collapse = " + ")))
    coxph(formula, data = data_complete)
  }
  
  models <- lapply(candidate_subsets, fit_model)
  
  # Build AIC results table
  results <- data.frame(
    variables    = sapply(candidate_subsets, function(x)
                          ifelse(length(x) == 0, "None (Forced Only)", 
                                 paste(x, collapse = ", "))),
    AIC          = round(sapply(models, AIC), 2),
    n_candidates = sapply(candidate_subsets, length)
  )
  
  results <- results[order(results$AIC), ]
  results$delta_AIC <- round(results$AIC - min(results$AIC), 2)
  
  # Clean simple table
  aic_table <- results %>%
    rename(
      "Additional Covariates" = variables,
      "No. of Candidates"     = n_candidates,
      "AIC"                   = AIC,
      "Delta AIC"             = delta_AIC
    ) %>%
    kable(format = "html", row.names = FALSE, align = c("l", "c", "c", "c"),
          caption = paste("All-Subsets AIC Results —", sex_label)) %>%
    kable_styling(
      bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"),
      full_width        = FALSE,
      position          = "center",
      font_size         = 13
    )
  
  # Save as png
  filename <- paste0("aic_table_", tolower(sex_label), ".png")
  save_kable(aic_table, file = filename, zoom = 2)
  cat("\nTable saved as:", filename, "\n")
  
  print(aic_table)
  
  # LRT: best model vs forced only
  best_index      <- which.min(sapply(models, AIC))
  best_candidates <- candidate_subsets[[best_index]]
  
  forced_model <- coxph(Surv(stroke_time, stroke_10yr) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES,
                         data = data_complete)
  
  best_model <- coxph(as.formula(paste("Surv(stroke_time, stroke_10yr) ~",
                                        paste(c(forced, best_candidates), collapse = " + "))),
                       data = data_complete)
  
  cat("\n===", sex_label, "- LRT: Best Model vs Forced Only ===\n")
  print(anova(forced_model, best_model))
  
  return(list(results       = results, 
              models        = models, 
              subsets       = candidate_subsets,
              data_complete = data_complete,
              best_model    = best_model,
              forced_model  = forced_model))
}


# Run for each sex
results_female <- run_variable_selection(female_data, "Female")
results_male   <- run_variable_selection(male_data,   "Male")

```






```{r, echo = F}
# Creating table 1 for report
summary_data <- frmgham_data_subset %>%
  select(SEX, stroke_10yr, DIABETES, SYSBP, AGE, PREVCHD, BPMEDS, CURSMOKE, TOTCHOL, BMI)

# Renaming ADH, RACE, EDUCBAS, and SMOKE for table
summary_data <- summary_data %>%
  mutate(DIABETES = factor(DIABETES,
                           levels = c(0,1),
                           labels = c("No", "Yes")),
         PREVCHD = factor(PREVCHD,
                          levels = c(0,1),
                          labels = c("Free of Disease",
                                     "Prevalent Disease")),
         BPMEDS = factor(BPMEDS,
                         levels = c(0,1),
                         labels = c("Not Currently Taken",
                                    "Currently Taken")),
         CURSMOKE = factor(CURSMOKE,
                           levels = c(0,1),
                           labels = c("Not Current Smoker",
                                      "Current Smoker")),
         stroke_10yr = factor(stroke_10yr,
                              levels = c(0,1),
                              labels = c("No Stroke", "Stroke")))


# Creating summary table
descriptive_table <- tbl_summary(summary_data,
                                 by = stroke_10yr,
                                 missing = "ifany",
                                 type = list(DIABETES ~ "categorical"),
                                 statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                                                  all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"),
                                 label = list(DIABETES ~ "Diabetes",
                                              SYSBP ~ "Systolic Blood Pressure (mmHg)",
                                              AGE ~ "Age (years)",
                                              PREVCHD ~ "Coronary Heart Disease",
                                              BPMEDS ~ "Taking Blood Pressure Medication",
                                              TOTCHOL ~ "Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)",
                                              CURSMOKE ~ "Smoking Status",
                                              BMI ~ "BMI (kg/m^2)")) %>%
  add_overall() %>%
  modify_table_body(~ .x %>%
                      dplyr::mutate(label = ifelse(label == "Unknown", "Missing", label)))

# Save table as image for report
gtsave(as_gt(descriptive_table), "descriptivetable_finalreport.png")

```
